book_metadata.title: Hookonomics: Guide to Hook
book_metadata.author: Himanshu Gandotra
book_metadata.domain: Content creation / Attention engineering / Social media marketing
book_metadata.core_thesis: Content success is determined by the ability to capture attention in the first few seconds using psychological hooks.

attention_economy.description: In the social media era attention is the most scarce resource. Content competes primarily for viewer attention.
attention_economy.implication: The first 3 seconds of content determine whether a viewer continues watching or scrolls away.

hook_definition.description: A hook is an attention trigger designed to stop scrolling and create curiosity or emotional engagement.

three_second_rule.description: The first three seconds determine viewer retention trajectory.
three_second_rule.goal: Move viewer attention sequentially from 3 seconds → 5 seconds → 10 seconds → 30 seconds → completion.

attention_pipeline.failure_condition: If the hook fails the rest of the content is never consumed.

hook_categories.description: Hooks that trigger primitive survival instincts in the brain.
Hooks targeting identity, belonging, and hierarchy psychology.
viewer_reactions: I want to belong to this group
I disagree and want to respond
I want to be like this person

hook_categories.description: Hooks using storytelling, structure, and pattern interruption to maintain engagement.

curiosity_gap_mechanics.definition: A curiosity gap occurs when information is partially revealed, forcing the brain to seek closure.
curiosity_gap_mechanics.psychology: Humans dislike missing information and feel compelled to resolve uncertainty.

curiosity_gap_templates: This is not the reason your ___ is failing
You're skipping the step that actually makes ___ work
You don't need more ___, you need better ___
This thing looks useless but it changes everything
You won't understand why this works until you try it
You're not solving the problem, you're protecting it
The most important part of ___ is the part you ignore
The thing you're trying to fix isn't broken
Stop asking how, start asking why
Your effort isn't the problem, your direction is
You think the secret is ___ but it's actually ___

hardcore_curiosity_hooks.template: You're optimizing the only part that doesn't matter
The mistake is not what you're doing, it's what you're avoiding
This doesn't make you fail, it makes you fail slowly
You're not improving, you're getting more stuck creatively
Your success is being delayed by something that feels productive
The problem isn't big, that's the problem
Your plan isn't wrong, it's too safe
You're doing the right thing at the wrong intensity
It worked. That's the worst part
Your biggest skill is causing your biggest problem
You didn't fail. You succeeded at the wrong thing
You don't lack talent, you lack the right boredom
You're not stuck, you're repeating progress that leads nowhere
You don't need new ideas, you need to escape old ones
Your growth stopped the moment you started measuring it
You're not overthinking, you're questioning the wrong decision
The thing you think is slowing you is protecting you
The skill you want is locked behind the skill you avoid

visual_hooks.description: Visual elements that trigger attention before the script begins.

hook_combination_strategy.insight: Top creators combine multiple hook categories.

example_combined_hook.text: Everyone teaches editing but nobody teaches attention.

key_takeaways: The first three seconds determine viewer retention.
Hooks must trigger curiosity or emotion instantly.
Top creators combine brain, social, and narrative hooks.
Content quality matters only after attention is captured.
